data.subject: Eboards Margin Recovery On Future Orders

emails.body:
Hi Tom, Kathy and Roger,

Gentle reminder as we approach the eBoards renewal, to bake/recover +U$2K/mo as we’re losing money on Imperva.

Thanks,

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: September 11, 2025 4:43 PM
To: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; Kathy Wu <Kathy.Wu@aptum.com>; Roger Gonzales <Roger.Gonzales@aptum.com>
Cc: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>; Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>; Carlton Currey <Carlton.Currey@aptum.com>; Noe Ibarra <Noe.Ibarra@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: re: eBOARDs Margin Recovery on Future Orders

Hi Tom & Kathy,

Any future upgrades or new orders for this customer must carry strong margins to offset the losses on the Imperva product. We’re targeting a recovery of +U$2K/mo.

eBoards has upcoming MCP and PenTest requirements, be sure to blend additional margins and let me know if we secure any deals.

Hi Roger,

As discussed, if we’re unable to offset margins, we need to revisit contracts (next year) to either a) increase rates or b) offer an alternative strategy (CloudFlare or other).

Thanks,

Notes:

eBoards Imperva margins 8%, our target is 35%

Good customer relationship, but lots of volatility towards Imperva product. Price disruption will risk churn.

eBoards has 3 Imperva contracts: $1K Sep ’26, $3K Nov ‘26 and $5K Oct ’27 (co

So each of these requests were sent into deal desk wouldn’t the 35% be added to each of them. I realize some of it could be degraded due to price increases on the Imperva side, but I don’t understand how it’s sitting where it’s sitting. All the prices I submitted were approved. Just thinking out loud sorry

Roger Gonzales

Account Manager

+1 (210.243.3807)
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: Monday, March 16, 2026 4:35:55 PM
To: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; Kathy Wu <Kathy.Wu@aptum.com>; Roger Gonzales <Roger.Gonzales@aptum.com>

+@Perkins, Ciara @Neumeier, Debra

Hi Matthew,

This is fantastic news~ Thank you for choosing to move forward with TD SYNNEX and for giving us the opportunity to earn your business. We are genuinely excited to support you & your team and ensure a smooth transition.

Rupesh, Maz, and I will remain fully engaged and dedicated to supporting all your needs for the Canadian region. In the US, Ciara Perkins (Business Development Rep, AWS) and Deb Neumeier (Sr. Sales Manager) will be your go-to contacts, and they're ready to provide the white glove service Aptum needs to be fully onboarded. Ciara and Deb, I'm looping you in here so you can assist Matthew and the team in getting set up and operational in the US (CTA is requested for March 22). Your support will be invaluable in ensuring a seamless experience for everyone involved. Rupesh will send a separate email with the background information to get you both fully up to speed on this opportunity.

In the meantime, I suggest we push ahead in Canada in getting the Aptum account activated and ready for future Canadian business. This way, you'll have everything in place and can hit the ground running as you expand.

Thanks again, Matthew—we truly appreciate the partnership and look forward to continuing a successful journey together.

Christine Thomson

Senior Director, Hyperscalers & ISVs

TD SYNNEX

6911 Creditview Road

Mississauga, Ontario

(416) 277-4566

Christine.Thomson@tdsynnex.com

www.linkedin.com/in/christinethomson1

@Lacie Allen-Morley, @Erik Tanguay, @Andrei Ianouchkevitch

We are planning a transition of US (not any other regions right now) customers from Ingram to TD Synnex.

@Andrei Ianouchkevitch, your customers will be the ones that are most impacted (because it's migrating their billing and taking away their—if they even use it—access to cloudcheckr).

@Lacie Allen-Morley, we will need someone from your team (maybe Kristina?) to lead the X's and O's on this because we need to submit documents for EACH customer in the US region to get them migrated off Ingram.

& @Erik Tanguay - You're probably going to need to be involved from a billing perspective at the beginning/end of the month.

---

I suggest we get us 5 (plus Kristina if she's the PM) in the same room and get what needs to be done and by when ASAP.

I'll book the call to get everyone on the same page.

Adam Nelson

Senior Director, Service Delivery

Hey Team,

We do have a request coming in for updated information for Digital Bridge. For each of the below please indicate timing to which you can provide the requested information if you can’t provide it by EOD tomorrow. I tagged who can provide the info to the best of my knowledge, but feel free to collaborate on these.

Details on top 10 clients, including MRR (@Paul Billard), scope (what LOB @Paul Billard), contract expiry (@Adam Nelson and @Kathy Wu or @Tom Kelsch) and margin (For margin let’s use the contribution margin here please and add a note to what is the contribution margin (@Paul Billard and @Dennis Lam)

Also include customer demographics (e.g. Industry and region) @Paul Billard and @Tom Kelsch please collaborate on this

% of revenue on contract vs month-to-month and average remaining contract term @Paul Billard you can leverage the Run Rate file and the renewal tracker.

Current booked backlog and sales pipeline @Tom Kelsch please get us the pipeline by MRC versus NRC and the LOB, TCV etc. Excel format please. @Kathy Wu@Paul Billard current booked backlog would be all closed won opportunities for MRC and NRC and what is left on the contract or left to be delivered on. Again the renewal tracker can be useful here.

Historical capex outlay for previous 3-5 years for maintenance, success-based capex and any facilities investments (will require any capex we financed via capital lease as I requested be added to the historical master file @Dennis Lam

Expected cap

HI Sarah,

For contribution margin, I only have the high level general margins by LOB.

If DB wants it per customer's MRR, I think utilizing the profitability data Jorge put together will be best. And contribution margin would be Revenues less any direct costs associated with customer, excluding rent and other overheads that isnt captured by Jorge.

Dennis Lam

Manager, Finance

+1 (604) 909-6386

From: Melvin Ching
Sent: Friday, February 20, 2026 4:50 PM
To: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Cc: Nedim Deovic <Nedim.Deovic@aptum.com>; Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>
Subject: 2026 annual security and compliance vendor review - PagerDuty

Good afternoon Adam,

As part of our vendor management program, in-scope vendors are required to be reviewed from a security and compliance perspective. This is for your vendor – PagerDuty.

To assist, I have prepared a report based on information from vendor compliance reports such as SOC 2 and/or other reports available (ISO 27001, PCI, etc).

Please review the report and complete Appendix B with your comments or simply reply back to this email with your comments/approval.

Although out of scope of our compliance program requirements, I would encourage you to conduct a full annual business review (financials, agreement, SLAs, etc.) of this vendor. If you decide to do so, please engage @Nedim Deovic and he can assist you.

In addition, @Fred Taylor-Young is also available to discuss any specific security issues that may be present in Aptum’s use of PagerDuty. If there are any, please feel free to add it to the comments in the report.

If you have any questions plea

data.subject: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - Sql Server License Verification

emails.body:
To my understanding - Aptum will be held liable and fined if we are audited by Microsoft.

I’m not aware of any ‘abuse’ to be reported except to the client which has already been done.

Barbara Krzywoszanski

Service Delivery Manager

+1 (514) 673-7542
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>
Date: Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 2:27 PM
To: Elizabeth Perez <Elizabeth.Perez@aptum.com>, Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>, Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>, Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>, Charles Rutledge <Charles.Rutledge@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification

This has not been updated in the ticket.

I had no clue we had an Abuse "department".

Who can confirm if this has been sent to Abuse? As I would expect this to be shown in the ticket, I would assume not. But how can we confirm?

Frederic Gingras

Senior Director, Account Management

m: +1 514-913-9153 teams: +1 514-673-7553
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Elizabeth Perez <Elizabeth.Perez@aptum.com>
Sent: March 12, 2026 1:52 PM
To: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>; Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Charles Rutledge <Charles.Rutledge@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification

Hi Fred,

Yes, it's been lingering for

Including @Fred Taylor-Young because you guys are talking about abuse.

I don't know if this falls under our "abuse" team; usually that is for people who are mis-using our products and services (running bitcoin mining or illegal websites with our servers..things like that).. but I'll let Fred TY take a quick look.

Adam Nelson

Senior Director, Service Delivery

+1 (438) 802-9796
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>
Sent: March 16, 2026 08:51
To: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>; Elizabeth Perez <Elizabeth.Perez@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Charles Rutledge <Charles.Rutledge@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification

To my understanding - Aptum will be held liable and fined if we are audited by Microsoft.

I’m not aware of any ‘abuse’ to be reported except to the client which has already been done.

Barbara Krzywoszanski

Service Delivery Manager

+1 (514) 673-7542
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>
Date: Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 2:27 PM
To: Elizabeth Perez <Elizabeth.Perez@aptum.com>, Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>, Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>, Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>, Charles Rutledge <Charles.Rutledge@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License

When the previous manager of Abuse (Joseph) was exited last year, a lot of historical knowledge was lost. It's also worth clarifying that he was a 1-man team; he had no reports. His Abuse responsibilities were given to InfoSec to handle as best we can, and since then, we have been trying to document some of that knowledge based on our interpretation of what Abuse should handle, which does align with what Adam said: the mis-use of our services.

I don't believe license (mis-)use should fall under the purview of Abuse. If a customer has installed or is using software and violating the license agreement, should Aptum be policing that at all? If we misinterpret a license agreement, couldn't that make Aptum liable? (I'm not saying Charles has misinterpreted it in this ticket, more as a general thought).

This ticket likely slipped through the cracks during the transition periods mentioned above, and also because, like IT, the InfoSec team don't typically look at the "Aptum" project in Jira - we have dedicated Jira projects for Security work.

Cheers,

Fred

Fred Taylor-Young

Senior Manager, IT & Security

Hi All,

Microsoft Azure invoices have been released as of March 15th. Future Azure invoices will be released on the 15th of the month when possible.

@Jorge Quintero Azure GP batches will be following the format AZUREMMYYYY (e.g. AZURE032026) to differentiate them from MO365 services

MO365 invoices will still be released at the end of the month.

Regards,
